title: Tokenizer Training Workflow
concept: Train the tokenizer on your corpus first, verify vocabulary coverage, then lock it before training the neural network. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Vocabulary Size Tradeoffs.'

exercises.prompt: When should the tokenizer's vocabulary be finalized and locked?

options.text: While the neural network is training
After the neural network is fully trained

exercises.explain: The tokenizer must compile and lock the vocabulary list before the neural network can start learning what those token IDs mean.

cards.title: Preparation First!
cards.body: Before a chef starts cooking, they chop all the ingredients and place them in bowls. You can't chop veggies while the soup is already boiling! Similarly, you must finish training your tokenizer and locking your vocabulary before you feed data to the AI.

cards.title: The Tokenizer Workflow
cards.body: Tokenizer training is the phase where you feed a text corpus to a tokenizer model (like BPE) so it can discover the most frequent word pieces. This generates a vocabulary. You must complete this and lock the vocabulary before training your neural network model.

cards.title: The Flashcard Builder
cards.body: Imagine teaching a toddler new words. You read them a custom storybook (your corpus) and write down every unique word they learn onto flashcards (the vocabulary). You must finish making all the flashcards first before you start testing how well they can read full books!

cards.title: Medical AI Preparation
cards.body: If you want to train a chat bot on medical texts, you first run the tokenizer on thousands of medical journals. It learns specialized tokens like "cardi-" and "-ology". Once it builds this vocabulary, you freeze it, and only then do you feed the tokenized texts to train the AI.

cards.title: Not a Team Sport
cards.body:
Myth: The tokenizer and the neural network learn together at the exact same time.
Reality: They are separate! You train the tokenizer first to build the dictionary, lock it, and only then do you train the neural network using those fixed tokens.

cards.title: Two-Phase Workflow
cards.body: Understanding the workflow helps avoid breaking the neural network's translation map.

left.text: Runs on corpus, builds vocabulary, freezes tokens.

right.text: Reads frozen tokens, learns associations, updates weights.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the correct order of the tokenizer training workflow?

options.text: Train tokenizer -> Verify vocabulary -> Lock vocabulary -> Train model
Train model -> Lock vocabulary -> Train tokenizer
Train tokenizer and model at the exact same time
Lock vocabulary -> Train model -> Build corpus

exercises.explain: First, you collect your corpus. Second, you train the tokenizer to build the vocabulary. Third, you verify the vocabulary. Finally, you freeze it and train the neural network.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Once neural network training begins, you must not change or update the tokenizer's vocabulary.
exercises.explain: If you change the tokenizer after training the neural network, the word IDs will shift, rendering the AI's learned weights useless.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing step of the tokenizer workflow.
exercises.sentence: First, train the tokenizer on the corpus to build the vocabulary, lock it, and then train the model.
exercises.explain: We run the tokenizer on the raw training corpus first to build our vocabulary list before feeding tokens to the neural network.

exercises.prompt: Two engineers are discussing their model training setup. Spot the one who is using the correct workflow.

options.text: "Our AI updates the tokenizer dynamically to add new words during training."
"We ran the tokenizer on our dataset, froze the vocabulary, and then started training the model."
"We train the neural network first, then build the tokenizer vocabulary from its outputs."

exercises.explain: Generating new tokens during model training would change the ID mapping, confusing the neural network. The vocabulary must remain frozen during network training.

exercises.prompt: Why must we absolutely lock the tokenizer's vocabulary before training the neural network?

options.text: Because the neural network maps its learning to fixed word ID numbers.
Because tokenizers train much slower than neural networks.
Because the tokenizer API charges per update.
Because it reduces the size of the training corpus.

exercises.explain: Locking your vocabulary before training is critical. If your tokenizer's vocabulary changes midway, the neural network will mismatch its learned word IDs and output absolute nonsense. Next up: Vocabulary Size Tradeoffs.
